title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Risk Brief: Literal Risk
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates literal risk increment in a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Ratchet strictness without permanently exempting legacy areas.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
